children.heading: Discontinuance of regulation by the Commission
children.label: a Discontinuance of regulation by the Commission

children.text: protects the public interest, the purposes listed in paragraph (2), and the environment to the same extent provided by licensing and regulation by the Commission under this subchapter and other applicable Federal laws, including the Endangered Species Act ( et seq.) and the Fish and Wildlife Coordination Act ( et seq.);16 U.S.C. 153116 U.S.C. 661

children.label: A
children.text: energy conservation;

children.label: B
children.text: the protection, mitigation of damage to, and enhancement of, fish and wildlife (including related spawning grounds and habitat);

children.label: C
children.text: the protection of recreational opportunities;

children.label: D
children.text: the preservation of other aspects of environmental quality;

children.label: E
children.text: the interests of Alaska Natives; and

children.label: F
children.text: other beneficial public uses, including irrigation, flood control, water supply, and navigation; and

children.label: A
children.text: the construction, maintenance, and operation by a licensee at its own expense of such lights and signals as may be directed by the Secretary of the Department in which the Coast Guard is operating, and such fishways as may be prescribed by the Secretary of the Interior or the Secretary of Commerce, as appropriate;

children.label: B
children.text: the operation of any navigation facilities which may be constructed as part of any project to be controlled at all times by such reasonable rules and regulations as may be made by the Secretary of the Army; and

children.label: C
children.text: except as provided in subsection (j), conditions for the protection, mitigation, and enhancement of fish and wildlife based on recommendations received pursuant to the Fish and Wildlife Coordination Act ( et seq.) from the National Marine Fisheries Service, the United States Fish and Wildlife Service, and State fish and wildlife agencies.16 U.S.C. 661

children.heading: Election of State licensing
children.label: c Election of State licensing
children.text: In the case of nonqualifying project works that would be a qualifying project works but for the fact that the project has been licensed (or exempted from licensing) by the Commission prior to , the licensee of such project may in its discretion elect to make the project subject to licensing and regulation by the State of Alaska under this section.November 9, 2000

children.heading: Project works on Federal lands
children.label: d Project works on Federal lands

children.text: the approval of the Secretary having jurisdiction over such lands; and
such conditions as the Secretary may prescribe.

children.heading: Consultation with affected agencies
children.label: e Consultation with affected agencies
children.text: The Commission shall consult with the Secretary of the Interior, the Secretary of Agriculture, and the Secretary of Commerce before certifying the State of Alaska’s regulatory program.

children.heading: Application of Federal laws
children.label: f Application of Federal laws
children.text: Nothing in this section shall preempt the application of Federal environmental, natural resources, or cultural resources protection laws according to their terms.

children.heading: Oversight by the Commission
children.label: g Oversight by the Commission
children.text: The State of Alaska shall notify the Commission not later than 30 days after making any significant modification to its regulatory program. The Commission shall periodically review the State’s program to ensure compliance with the provisions of this section.

children.heading: Resumption of Commission authority
children.label: h Resumption of Commission authority
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (a), the Commission shall reassert its licensing and regulatory authority under this subchapter if the Commission finds that the State of Alaska has not complied with one or more of the requirements of this section.

Upon application by the Governor of the State of Alaska, the Commission shall within 30 days commence a review of the State of Alaska’s regulatory program for water-power development to determine whether it complies with the requirements of subsection (a).

The Commission’s review required by paragraph (1) shall be completed within 1 year of initiation, and the Commission shall within 30 days thereafter issue a final order determining whether or not the State of Alaska’s regulatory program for water-power development complies with the requirements of subsection (a).

If the Commission fails to issue a final order in accordance with paragraph (2) the State of Alaska’s regulatory program for water-power development shall be deemed to be in compliance with subsection (a).

children.heading: Fish and wildlife
children.label: j Fish and wildlife
children.text: If the State of Alaska determines that a recommendation under subsection (a)(3)(C) is inconsistent with paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection (a), the State of Alaska may decline to adopt all or part of the recommendations in accordance with the procedures established under .section 803(j)(2) of this title
